So the negatives are in the order they appeared in the original array, but the positives are in their original order. Wait, no. Wait the output has the negatives first, then the positives. But the original array has -1, then -3, then -7. So the negatives are in the order they appeared in the input. So the function should collect all negatives in the order they appear, then the positives in their original order.

So the approach would be to iterate through the array, collect all negative numbers in the order they appear, then collect the positive numbers in their original order. Then combine them.

So the function can be written as follows:

def re_arrange_array(arr, n):
    negatives = [x for x in arr if x < 0]
    positives = [x for x in arr if x >= 0]
    return negatives + positives

arguments.answer:
def re_arrange_array(arr, n):
    negatives = [x for x in arr if x < 0]
    positives = [x for x in arr if x >= 0]
    return negatives + positives
